word: G1877 - ἐπανάγω - epanágō - ep-an-ag'-o - Launch out

sections.paragraph:
- Original: επανάγω

- Transliteration: Epanago

- Phonetic: ep-an-ag'-o

- Definition:

1. to lead up upon

a. a ship upon the deep, to put out

2. to lead back

3. to return

- Origin: from G1909 and G321

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G1909 and G321; to lead up on that is (technically) to put out (to sea); (intransitively) to return: - launch (thrust) out return.
